purpose: settings.graph_attribute_parsing left at its default True: load_graph consumes the knob at load time (pyreason.py:602), converting the node attribute special=1 and edge attribute rel=1 into 'graph-attribute-fact' facts with bound [1,1] — no add_fact anywhere — so the rule derives derived(B) at t=1 and t=2, and its regrounding keeps max_rules_time ahead of t so the perfect-convergence exit (interpretation.py:674-680) never fires: get_time reads 3 and each filter probe carries three frames; the attribute facts' own trace rows stay suppressed by default-False save_graph_attributes_to_trace (interpretation.py:297/1538), leaving nodes-special/edges-rel all-empty. The twin graph-attr-parsing-off zeroes every reasoning probe; only trace-edge digest-equals across the pair (verified at authoring — the harness judges each case within one knob position) — the default-true-parse and read-at-load-time classes.
provenance: spine case against docs/surface.md setting:graph_attribute_parsing default-true-parse + read-at-load-time
